comments.body: @rhatdan @nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat we need this in before we can update libpod and CRI-O.
Travis is not behaving with all the network flakes... meh
Force pushed in hope to get some fresh Travis nodes.

:pushpin: Commit bfeb021 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan bfeb0211dd7adef269d5507582636588fe218687 -->
:hourglass: Testing commit bfeb021 with merge e7f2e27...

> @QiWang19 We need buildah login updated to use keyring and then we can make a full release.

Note that we cannot update libpod and cri-o without this change. Anything we want for the next release would potentially delay the update unless we'd merge a non-release commit of buildah.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat can we please force merge? The registries are flaky at the moment and I am restarting tests for around two hours now :-D
@rhatdan, ack on the new release.  I'll bump to 1.10 unless I hear otherwise.
Just checked with @QiWang19 , the keyring PR is on the libpod side.  We should also add it to Buildah, but I wouldn't hold 1.10 for it.   https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3674
@vrothberg I've just started a retest on the one failing test.  In the mean time I'll go run some tests by hand.  Will merge by hand if still necessary afterwards.
Passes baseline tests.  Once the travis job wraps up, I'll merge.
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
Finally past travis, off to build 1.10.

> Just checked with @QiWang19 , the keyring PR is on the libpod side. We should also add it to Buildah, but I wouldn't hold 1.10 for it. [containers/libpod#3674](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3674)

sure. I just create this PR in buildah https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1758. we can get this in after 1.10
